steps.title: Substitute the given y-coordinate into both equations
steps.content:
The intersection point is $(q, 19)$, so $y = 19$ satisfies both equations. Substituting:

$$-x - w(19) = -337 \implies x + 19w = 337$$
$$2x - w(19) = 47$$

Now we have a system of two equations in two unknowns ($x$ and $w$).

steps.title: Add the equations to eliminate w
steps.content:
Add the two equations to cancel the $19w$ terms:

$$(x + 19w) + (2x - 19w) = 337 + 47$$
$$3x = 384$$
$$x = 128$$
